title: heuchera glabella var. suksdorfii
intro: Heuchera glabella var. suksdorfii, also known as a variety of coral bells, is a perennial plant species in the saxifrage family native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Heuchera glabella var. suksdorfii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Heuchera glabella var. suksdorfii can be prepared as a salve or infusion for topical use, or as a decoction for internal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
